results.rendered_html:
1. apāka (von añc mit apa) adj. abseits oder hinten liegend, entfernt, von fern kommend: ābhogayaṃ pra yadicchanta aitanāpākāḥ prāñco mama kecidāpayaḥ  ṚV. 1,110,2 . adidyutatsvapāko vibhāvā 6,11,4 . 12,2 .  VS. 20,44 . Die Erklärer zerlegen das Wort in a + pāka (!).
— Vgl. apākā, apākacakṣas, apākāt, apākestha.

results.rendered_html:
apāka a.
1 Ved. [fr. aṃc with apa] (a) Situated aside or behind. (b) Remote, distant. (c) Coming from a distant place. (d) Unequalled, incomparable, very great (analpa  Sāy. ); ˚cakzus˚cakzas of unequalled brightness; ˚kāt-kā aside, distant; ˚kestha standing behind.
2 Raw, unripe.
3 Not matured, undigested.
4 Of matured intellect, wise; (pākaḥ paktavyaprajñaḥ mūrkhaḥ tadvilakṣaṇaḥ)  Rv. 1. 110. 2 , 6. 12. 2. 
— kaḥ
1 Indigestion (of food &c.).
2 Immaturity, not being ripe or cooked.
Comp.
— ja a.
1 not produced by cooking or ripening.
2 natural, original;  Bhāṣā P. 42 , 95. 
— śākaṃ ginger.
